intel.label: Intel
intel.description: Founded by Fairchild refugees Gordon Moore and Robert Noyce. Invented the microprocessor (4004) in 1971, enabling personal computers. Their x86 architecture dominated PCs for decades.

amd.label: AMD
amd.description: Advanced Micro Devices, founded by Jerry Sanders from Fairchild. Survived as Intel\

nvidia.label: NVIDIA
nvidia.description: Founded by Jensen Huang to revolutionize graphics. The GeForce GPU conquered gaming, then CUDA unlocked parallel computing. Now the world\

arm_ltd.label: Arm Holdings
arm_ltd.description: Spun out from Acorn Computers in Cambridge, UK. Their RISC architecture prioritizes power efficiency over raw speed. Licensed by Apple, Qualcomm, and Samsung, Arm powers 99% of smartphones. Now expanding into servers and AI Edge devices.

samsung.label: Samsung
samsung.description: South Korean conglomerate that evolved from trading to electronics. World leader in DRAM memory, NAND flash, and smartphone displays. Their foundry competes with TSMC. From semiconductors to smartphones, Samsung shapes the digital world.

qualcomm.label: Qualcomm
qualcomm.description: San Diego company that pioneered CDMA mobile technology. Their Snapdragon chips power most Android phones, integrating CPU, GPU, and 5G modem. Collects royalties on nearly every smartphone sold through their essential wireless patents.

broadcom.label: Broadcom
broadcom.description: Started as a fabless semiconductor company, now a $500B giant. Dominates networking chips, Wi-Fi, and enterprise storage. Acquired VMware, Symantec, and CA Technologies. Their chips connect virtually every device to the internet.

hp.label: Hewlett-Packard
hp.description: The founding company of Silicon Valley, started in a Palo Alto garage by Stanford engineers Bill Hewlett and Dave Packard. Pioneered test equipment, calculators, and printers. HP Labs invented technologies from HP-UX to the inkjet. Split into HP Inc. and HPE in 2015.

sun_micro.label: Sun Microsystems
sun_micro.description: Stanford University Network graduates built powerful UNIX workstations. Created Java (

cisco.label: Cisco
cisco.description: Founded by Stanford couple who wanted their campus networks to talk to each other. Their routers and switches became the internet\

dji.label: DJI
dji.description: Da-Jiang Innovations, the Shenzhen company that created the consumer drone industry. Their Phantom and Mavic drones dominate 70% of the market. Made aerial photography accessible to everyone from filmmakers to farmers.

boston_dynamics.label: Boston Dynamics
boston_dynamics.description: MIT spinoff famous for mesmerizing robot videos. Spot the robot dog patrols factories, while Atlas performs parkour. Owned by Hyundai after stints with Google and SoftBank. Pushing the physical limits of robotics.

adobe.label: Adobe
adobe.description: PostScript founders John Warnock and Charles Geschke built the digital creative suite. Photoshop, Illustrator, and Premiere Pro are industry standards. Invented PDF. Pioneered SaaS with Creative Cloud subscription model.

sap.label: SAP
sap.description: German enterprise software giant that runs the world\

bytedance.label: ByteDance
bytedance.description: Chinese startup that mastered algorithmic content recommendation. TikTok became the fastest-growing app ever, addicting billions with short videos. Their

airbnb.label: Airbnb
airbnb.description: Air mattresses in a San Francisco apartment became a $100B hospitality network. Brian Chesky\

paypal_co.label: PayPal
paypal_co.description: The original fintech merger of Confinity and X.com. The

stripe_co.label: Stripe
stripe_co.description: Irish brothers Patrick and John Collison built payment infrastructure for developers.

spotify_co.label: Spotify
spotify_co.description: Swedish startup that saved the music industry by making piracy inconvenient. Daniel Ek convinced labels to stream. 500M+ users now pay for access over ownership. Pioneered podcast acquisitions and personalized playlists.

deepmind.label: Google DeepMind
deepmind.description: London-based AI lab founded by Demis Hassabis. AlphaGo defeated the world Go champion in 2016, a decade ahead of predictions. AlphaFold solved protein folding, revolutionizing biology. Acquired by Google for $500M, now merged with Google Brain.

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.description: AI research lab founded by Musk, Altman, and others to ensure AGI benefits humanity. GPT-3 and ChatGPT brought AI to mainstream consumers. DALL-E generates images, Whisper transcribes speech. The company that made AI a verb.

anthropic.label: Anthropic
anthropic.description: Founded by ex-OpenAI researchers Dario and Daniela Amodei, focused on AI safety. Claude assistant emphasizes being helpful, harmless, and honest. Constitutional AI methodology tries to make models self-correcting. The cautious alternative to OpenAI.

huggingface.label: Hugging Face
huggingface.description: The GitHub of machine learning. French-American startup that democratized AI with open-source Transformers library. Hosts 500K+ models and datasets. Any researcher can share, any company can deploy. Community-driven AI development.

sequoia.label: Sequoia Capital
sequoia.description: The legendary VC firm that backed Apple, Google, Oracle, PayPal, YouTube, Instagram, and NVIDIA. Don Valentine\

a16z.label: a16z
a16z.description: Andreessen Horowitz, founded by Marc Andreessen (Netscape) and Ben Horowitz.

xerox_parc.label: Xerox PARC
xerox_parc.description: The legendary Palo Alto Research Center invented the graphical user interface, mouse, ethernet, and laser printing. Steve Jobs famously visited in 1979, inspiring the Macintosh. Despite creating revolutionary tech, Xerox failed to commercialize it.

darpa.label: DARPA
darpa.description: The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency, created after Sputnik shocked America. Funded ARPANET (the internet\

mit_csail.label: MIT CSAIL
mit_csail.description: The MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, founded by Minsky and McCarthy. Birthplace of AI as an academic field, hacker culture, and the Free Software movement. Tim Berners-Lee and many AI pioneers worked here.

stanford_ai_lab.label: Stanford AI Lab
stanford_ai_lab.description: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, founded by John McCarthy (who coined

cern.label: CERN
cern.description: The European Organization for Nuclear Research, home of the Large Hadron Collider. Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web here in 1989 to share research data. Particle physics meets information technology.

tsmc.label: TSMC
tsmc.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company, founded by Morris Chang through government initiative. Invented the

asml.label: ASML
asml.description: Dutch company with a global monopoly on EUV lithography machines. Each machine costs $200M and uses plasma to print transistors smaller than a virus. Without ASML, no advanced chips exist. The most important company most people never heard of.

alan_turing.label: Alan Turing
alan_turing.description: British mathematician who defined computation with the Turing Machine and cracked Nazi Enigma codes at Bletchley Park. His 1950 paper \

claude_shannon.label: Claude Shannon
claude_shannon.description: Bell Labs mathematician who invented information theory in 1948. Proved all communication could be digitized into bits, defined channel capacity, and invented error-correcting codes. Also built juggling robots and rode unicycles through MIT halls. The Einstein of the information age.

william_shockley.label: William Shockley
william_shockley.description: Nobel laureate who co-invented the transistor at Bell Labs with Bardeen and Brattain. His toxic management of Shockley Semiconductor drove the \

john_mccarthy.label: John McCarthy
john_mccarthy.description: The mathematician who coined the term \

marvin_minsky.label: Marvin Minsky
marvin_minsky.description: MIT pioneer who co-founded the AI Lab and wrote \

dennis_ritchie.label: Dennis Ritchie
dennis_ritchie.description: Bell Labs computer scientist who created the C programming language and co-created Unix with Ken Thompson. C became the mother tongue of systems programming. Unix philosophy shaped all modern operating systems. Died the same week as Steve Jobs but received far less recognition.

tim_berners_lee.label: Tim Berners-Lee
tim_berners_lee.description: British scientist at CERN who invented the World Wide Web in 1989—HTML, HTTP, and URLs. Crucially, he gave it away free instead of patenting it. Knighted by the Queen, W3C founder. Now advocates for data privacy and fights the web\

yann_lecun.label: Yann LeCun
yann_lecun.description: French AI scientist who invented convolutional neural networks that let computers \

gordon_moore.label: Gordon Moore
gordon_moore.description: Intel co-founder who in 1965 predicted transistor density would double roughly every two years—Moore\

steve_jobs.label: Steve Jobs
steve_jobs.description: Apple co-founder who stood at the intersection of technology and liberal arts. Created Macintosh, was fired, bought Pixar, returned, then delivered iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. His obsession with design and user experience redefined consumer electronics. \

steve_wozniak.label: Steve Wozniak
steve_wozniak.description: The engineering genius who single-handedly designed the Apple I and II. Woz built the first personal computers that actually worked and that people wanted. Later focused on education and philanthropy. The anti-Jobs—humble, generous, and beloved by engineers. \

bill_gates.label: Bill Gates
bill_gates.description: Microsoft co-founder who saw that software, not hardware, would define computing. MS-DOS then Windows dominated PCs for decades. Ruthless competitor, antitrust target, then world\

larry_ellison.label: Larry Ellison
larry_ellison.description: Oracle founder who built a database empire through relentless salesmanship and ruthless acquisition. Yachts, fighter jets, and Japanese samurai culture define his style. Became world\

morris_chang.label: Morris Chang
morris_chang.description: Texas Instruments veteran who returned to Taiwan and founded TSMC in 1987. His \

linus_torvalds.label: Linus Torvalds
linus_torvalds.description: Finnish programmer who wrote Linux as a hobby while a university student. It now runs the internet, Android, and the cloud. Also created Git for version control. Famous for blunt code reviews and profane mailing list posts. Gave software freedom to the world by accident.

jeff_bezos.label: Jeff Bezos
jeff_bezos.description: Amazon founder who started with books and built the \

jensen_huang.label: Jensen Huang
jensen_huang.description: NVIDIA co-founder who bet the company on GPUs for gaming, then AI. The leather jacket, the keynotes, and the kitchen-table founding story are iconic. NVIDIA is now worth $3T+ as the AI revolution\

larry_page.label: Larry Page
larry_page.description: Google co-founder who invented PageRank by treating web links like academic citations. His vision to \

sergey_brin.label: Sergey Brin
sergey_brin.description: Google co-founder who emigrated from Russia age 6. His mathematical skills and Page\

reed_hastings.label: Reed Hastings
reed_hastings.description: Netflix co-founder who pivoted from DVD-by-mail to streaming before anyone else saw it coming. Built a content empire with \

elon_musk.label: Elon Musk
elon_musk.description: Serial founder of PayPal, Tesla, SpaceX, Neuralink, Boring Company, and xAI. Made EVs desirable, landed rockets, and deploys Starlink globally. Bought Twitter/X. World\

mark_zuckerberg.label: Mark Zuckerberg
mark_zuckerberg.description: Facebook founder who connected 3 billion people from a Harvard dorm room. Acquired Instagram and WhatsApp. \

jack_ma.label: Jack Ma
jack_ma.description: Former English teacher who founded Alibaba in his Hangzhou apartment. Built China\

pony_ma.label: Pony Ma
pony_ma.description: Tencent founder who built WeChat, the super-app that is China\

patrick_collison.label: Patrick Collison
patrick_collison.description: Irish prodigy who built Stripe with brother John from their MIT dorm. Seven lines of code to accept payments. Built developer-first infrastructure for internet commerce worth $65B+. Also runs research labs in science funding and progress studies. The builder\

satoshi_nakamoto.label: Satoshi Nakamoto
satoshi_nakamoto.description: Pseudonymous creator of Bitcoin who solved the double-spend problem for digital currency using blockchain. Published the whitepaper October 2008, launched Bitcoin January 2009, disappeared 2011. Identity unknown—individual or group? Owns 1M inactive BTC. The most influential ghost ever.

vitalik_buterin.label: Vitalik Buterin
vitalik_buterin.description: Russian-Canadian programmer who conceived Ethereum at age 19. Built a \

tim_cook.label: Tim Cook
tim_cook.description: Apple CEO who succeeded Steve Jobs and proved the doubters wrong. Tripled revenue, reached $3T valuation. Operations genius who mastered supply chains. Quietly gay CEO of world\

satya_nadella.label: Satya Nadella
satya_nadella.description: Microsoft CEO who transformed the company from Windows-centric to cloud-first. Azure rivals AWS, GitHub powers developers, OpenAI partnership puts AI in everything. \

sundar_pichai.label: Sundar Pichai
sundar_pichai.description: Indian-born CEO of Alphabet and Google. Rose through Chrome and Android leadership. Calm, diplomatic, navigating antitrust suits and AI competition. Pushed Bard/Gemini to compete with ChatGPT. Low-key style contrasts with predecessors. Steering the world\

lisa_su.label: Lisa Su
lisa_su.description: AMD CEO who orchestrated one of tech\

demis_hassabis.label: Demis Hassabis
demis_hassabis.description: DeepMind co-founder, child chess prodigy, and game designer who now leads Google\

sam_altman.label: Sam Altman
sam_altman.description: OpenAI CEO who shipped ChatGPT and triggered the AI race. Previously ran Y Combinator age 28. Briefly fired then rehired in dramatic 2023 board chaos. Fundraising genius courting Microsoft and sovereign wealth. The CEO at the center of AI\

ilya_sutskever.label: Ilya Sutskever
ilya_sutskever.description: OpenAI co-founder and chief scientist behind GPT models. Student of Hinton who co-created AlexNet. Key figure in the dramatic 2023 board attempt to oust Sam Altman. Left to start Safe Superintelligence Inc. The scientist searching for both capability and alignment in AI.

don_valentine.label: Don Valentine
don_valentine.description: Founded Sequoia Capital in 1972, backing Apple, Cisco, Google, and YouTube. \

masayoshi_son.label: Masayoshi Son
masayoshi_son.description: SoftBank founder who rewrote venture rules with the $100B Vision Fund. Early bet on Alibaba returned $80B+. WeWork and other failures taught that not every bet pays. Believes in the \

marc_andreessen.label: Marc Andreessen
marc_andreessen.description: Netscape co-founder who made the web visual with Mosaic browser. \

reid_hoffman.label: Reid Hoffman
reid_hoffman.description: LinkedIn co-founder who built the professional social network. PayPal Mafia member, Greylock partner, AI early-adopter. Hosts podcasts, invests in AI startups, wrote \

paul_graham.label: Paul Graham
paul_graham.description: Y Combinator co-founder who industrialized startup creation. Lisp hacker, essayist, painter. Funded Airbnb, Dropbox, Stripe, Reddit. Essays like \

max_levchin.label: Max Levchin
max_levchin.description: PayPal co-founder who built the fraud-detection systems that saved the company. Ukrainian immigrant, cryptography expert, serial founder. Now CEO of Affirm (buy-now-pay-later). The PayPal Mafia technologist who keeps building instead of just investing. Still coding in his 40s.

roelof_botha.label: Roelof Botha
roelof_botha.description: South African-born Sequoia partner who went from PayPal CFO to the Valley\

transistor.label: The Transistor
transistor.description: The tiny electronic switch that replaced vacuum tubes. Invented at Bell Labs by Shockley, Bardeen, and Brattain. Billions of transistors now pack onto fingernail-sized chips, controlling electrical flow to perform calculations at incredible speeds.

rsa.label: RSA Encryption
rsa.description: The asymmetric encryption algorithm that secures the internet. Named after creators Rivest, Shamir, and Adleman, it uses the mathematical difficulty of factoring large prime numbers to protect passwords, credit cards, and private communications online.

lithography.label: Lithography
lithography.description: A photographic process using light to print incredibly tiny circuit patterns onto silicon wafers. Each generation shrinks transistors further, following Moore\

ic.label: Integrated Circuit
ic.description: Jack Kilby and Robert Noyce independently invented ways to put many transistors on a single chip. It solved the \

dram.label: DRAM
dram.description: Dynamic Random Access Memory invented by Robert Dennard at IBM. The computer\

optical_fiber.label: Optical Fiber
optical_fiber.description: Ultra-pure glass strands that transmit data as pulses of light over vast distances with minimal loss. Developed by Corning, they form the high-speed backbone of global internet and telecommunications, carrying 99% of international data traffic.

ethernet.label: Ethernet
ethernet.description: Invented by Bob Metcalfe at Xerox PARC, this protocol became the standard for connecting computers in local area networks. Its simple, robust design beat competitors like Token Ring. Still the foundation of wired networking, now reaching 400 Gbps speeds.

arpanet.label: ARPANET
arpanet.description: The Advanced Research Projects Agency Network, the direct ancestor of the modern internet. Funded by DARPA, it connected universities and research labs using packet switching. The first message sent in 1969 crashed after two letters. This experimental network proved that distributed communication could work and laid the groundwork for TCP/IP and the World Wide Web.

gps.label: GPS
gps.description: The Global Positioning System, a U.S. military satellite constellation providing precise location and timing anywhere on Earth. Made available for civilian use in 2000. Enables navigation apps, ride-sharing, precision agriculture, and synchronized financial trading.

optical_disc.label: Optical Disc
optical_disc.description: A digital storage format using lasers to read microscopic pits on a spinning disc. The Compact Disc revolutionized music distribution, followed by DVDs for video and Blu-ray for HD content. Peak technology before streaming took over in the 2010s.

tcp_ip.label: TCP/IP
tcp_ip.description: The Transmission Control Protocol and Internet Protocol stack that became the common language of the internet. Developed by Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn, it allows different networks to interconnect and ensures data packets reach their destination reliably.

arm_arch.label: ARM Architecture
arm_arch.description: A power-efficient RISC processor design from Cambridge that prioritizes energy efficiency over raw speed. Licensed to chip makers worldwide, ARM powers 99% of smartphones. Apple Silicon brought ARM to laptops and desktops with impressive performance.

cisco_router.label: Cisco Router
cisco_router.description: The traffic cop of the internet. Founded by Stanford couple Len Bosack and Sandy Lerner, Cisco routers direct data packets across networks to their destinations. Their equipment became the essential plumbing infrastructure of the global internet.

gsm.label: GSM
gsm.description: Global System for Mobile Communications, Europe\

mp3.label: MP3
mp3.description: The audio compression format developed by Fraunhofer Institute that shrinks music files to 1/10th their original size. It enabled Napster, iPod, and Spotify by making music portable and shareable. Disrupted the entire recording industry in the 2000s.

cmos.label: CMOS Sensor
cmos.description: Complementary Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor image sensors that convert light into digital signals. Invented by Eric Fossum, they replaced CCDs by offering lower power and on-chip processing. CMOS sensors turned every smartphone into a capable camera for billions.

blue_led.label: Blue LED
blue_led.description: The missing piece for full-color and white LED lighting, invented by Nakamura, Akasaki, and Amano (Nobel Prize 2014). Blue LEDs combine with red and green to create displays, and with phosphors for energy-efficient lighting that now saves billions in electricity.

bluetooth.label: Bluetooth
bluetooth.description: Named after Viking king Harald Bluetooth who united Denmark, this wireless standard unites devices. Developed by Ericsson, it connects headphones, keyboards, mice, and wearables over short distances. Now in billions of devices with Low Energy versions for IoT.

ssl_tls.label: SSL/TLS
ssl_tls.description: Secure Sockets Layer and Transport Layer Security protocols that encrypt internet communications. Developed at Netscape, they put the \

wifi.label: WiFi
wifi.description: Wireless Local Area Networking based on IEEE 802.11 standards. Freed computers from ethernet cables and enabled laptops, smartphones, and IoT devices to connect anywhere. Now essential infrastructure in homes, offices, and public spaces worldwide.

lidar.label: LiDAR
lidar.description: Light Detection and Ranging uses laser pulses to create precise 3D maps of surroundings. Essential for autonomous vehicles to \

lte.label: LTE (4G)
lte.description: Long Term Evolution, the 4th generation mobile network bringing true broadband speeds to smartphones. Enabled HD video streaming, video calls, and the app economy explosion. Global deployment made mobile internet a daily necessity for billions of users.

alexnet.label: AlexNet
alexnet.description: The deep neural network by Alex Krizhevsky, Ilya Sutskever, and Geoffrey Hinton that won ImageNet 2012 by a massive margin. Proved GPUs plus deep learning works for image recognition. Widely considered the \

hbm.label: HBM
hbm.description: High Bandwidth Memory stacks DRAM chips vertically with thousands of connections. Co-developed by AMD and SK Hynix, it feeds data to AI accelerators fast enough to keep GPUs busy. Essential for training large language models where memory bandwidth is the bottleneck.

gan.label: GAN
gan.description: Generative Adversarial Networks by Ian Goodfellow pit two neural networks against each other. One generates, one judges, both improve. Enabled photorealistic face generation, deepfakes, and inspired the creative AI revolution including Stable Diffusion.

5g_nr.label: 5G (New Radio)
5g_nr.description: Fifth generation mobile networks offering up to 20 Gbps speeds and millisecond latency. Enables real-time remote surgery, autonomous vehicles, and massive IoT deployments. Uses mmWave and sub-6GHz bands. Still rolling out globally with mixed coverage.

euv.label: EUV Lithography
euv.description: Extreme Ultraviolet Lithography uses 13.5nm wavelength light to print sub-7nm chip features. ASML\

stable_diffusion.label: Stable Diffusion
stable_diffusion.description: Open-source text-to-image AI model by Stability AI that runs on consumer GPUs. Democratized AI art creation by making it free and local. Sparked debates about artist rights and job displacement while enabling unprecedented creative tools for millions.

unix.label: Unix
unix.description: The operating system created at Bell Labs by Ken Thompson and Dennis Ritchie. Its philosophy of simple, modular tools connected by pipes influenced all modern OSes. Unix spawned BSD, Solaris, macOS, and inspired Linux. Powers most internet servers today.

c_lang.label: C Language
c_lang.description: Created by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs to rewrite Unix portably. C gives low-level hardware access with high-level abstraction. It became the foundation of operating systems, databases, and embedded systems. C++ and many languages descended from it.

sql.label: SQL
sql.description: Structured Query Language developed at IBM for relational databases. Its English-like syntax democratized data access. SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE became universal commands. Powers Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL, and every major database system worldwide.

javascript.label: JavaScript
javascript.description: Brendan Eich created it in 10 days at Netscape to make web pages interactive. Despite its quirks, it became the only language running in all browsers. Node.js brought it server-side. Now one of the world\

git.label: Git
git.description: Linus Torvalds created this distributed version control system in 2 weeks when Linux\

unity.label: Unity
unity.description: The game engine that democratized game development. Its accessible tools let indie developers create without large teams. Powers most mobile games and VR experiences. The \

toshiba_t1100.label: Toshiba T1100
toshiba_t1100.description: The first mass-market IBM PC-compatible laptop with clamshell design. Weighed 4kg and ran on batteries. Established the laptop form factor that billons use today. Proved portable computing could be practical for mobile professionals.

excel.label: Microsoft Excel
excel.description: The spreadsheet that conquered the business world. Originally for Mac, then dominated on Windows. VisiCalc invented the category, Lotus 1-2-3 popularized it, but Excel won with superior features. Still the language of finance and business analytics.

photoshop.label: Adobe Photoshop
photoshop.description: Created by Thomas and John Knoll, acquired by Adobe. \

mosaic.label: Mosaic Browser
mosaic.description: The first web browser to display images inline with text, developed at NCSA by Marc Andreessen and Eric Bina. Made the web visual and accessible. Its graphical interface sparked the internet boom. Andreessen later co-founded Netscape based on Mosaic.

netscape.label: Netscape Navigator
netscape.description: The browser that commercialized the World Wide Web. Its 1995 IPO sparked the dot-com boom. Fought and lost the browser war to Microsoft\

amazon_com.label: Amazon.com
amazon_com.description: Jeff Bezos started selling books from his Seattle garage, then became the \

paypal.label: PayPal
paypal.description: The merger of Confinity and Elon Musk\

skype.label: Skype
skype.description: The Estonian startup that made free video calls possible between computers. \

github.label: GitHub
github.description: The social network for code built around Git. Chris Wanstrath and Tom Preston-Werner made open source collaboration social. 100+ million developers. Microsoft acquired it for $7.5B. Now hosts most of the world\

stripe.label: Stripe
stripe.description: Irish brothers Patrick and John Collison built payment infrastructure that developers love. Seven lines of code to accept payments. Powers Amazon, Google, and most internet commerce. Valued at $65B+. The invisible plumbing making internet transactions just work.

zoom.label: Zoom
zoom.description: Eric Yuan left Cisco WebEx frustrated by poor quality. Built Zoom to \

cursor.label: Cursor
cursor.description: AI-first code editor that deeply integrates large language models into the development workflow. Built on VS Code foundation with custom AI features. Edit code through chat, inline completions, and codebase understanding. Part of the wave reimagining programming tools for the AI era.

illustrator.label: Adobe Illustrator
illustrator.description: The industry-standard vector graphics editor for logos, icons, typography, and illustrations. Unlike Photoshop\

premiere_pro.label: Adobe Premiere Pro
premiere_pro.description: Professional non-linear video editing software that brought Hollywood-quality editing to desktop computers. From indie YouTubers to major film productions, Premiere Pro handles everything from raw footage to final color grading. Deep integration with After Effects and Audition creates a complete post-production ecosystem.

nano_banana.label: Nano Banana
nano_banana.description: The viral AI image model built on Google\
